items.q: Why is the electric field between parallel charged plates considered uniform?
items.correct: Field strength is constant in magnitude and direction throughout the region (away from edges), unlike the inverse-square field of a point charge.
items.misconception: The field still decreases with the inverse square of distance — uniform just means it's smooth.
items.random_wrong: Because the plates are uniform in shape.
items.why: Students extrapolate inverse-square thinking everywhere; they don't distinguish point sources from extended geometries.

items.q: In the ideal gas equation pV = nRT, what must the temperature T be measured in?
items.misconception: Either Celsius or Kelvin gives the same result.
items.random_wrong: Whatever units make the numbers work out neatly.
items.why: Students plug in Celsius because that's their everyday scale, leading to nonsensical results at low temperatures where the difference is dramatic.

items.q: Why does light bend when it enters a denser medium like glass?
items.correct: Because it slows down — the change in speed at the boundary causes the wavefront to bend (towards the normal).
items.misconception: Because the medium is denser, it physically blocks part of the light and forces it to turn.
items.random_wrong: Because gravity in the glass pulls the light down.
items.why: Students attach refraction to 'density' as a material property without linking it to the change in wave speed and Snell's law.

items.correct: The driving frequency matches the natural frequency.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The driving frequency matches the natural frequency.. “Damping is critical.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Heavy damping in a car suspension causes:
items.correct: Slow return to equilibrium without oscillation.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Slow return to equilibrium without oscillation.. “Continuous oscillation at high amplitude.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
